prevalence.trend: increasing with population aging and improved detection

burden.mortalityRate: Indirect; elevated risk of progression to dementia

vulnerablePopulations: people with depression and social isolation
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Cognitive concern with objective impairment (1+ domains) and preserved functional independence; cognitive screening (MoCA/MMSE) plus workup for reversible causes; DSM-5/ICD-aligned criteria

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: limited; opportunistic screening in APS varies by municipality
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; risk-factor management can slow progression, but access to cognitive rehab is limited

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care recognition variable; specialist evaluation (neurology/geriatrics) concentrated in larger cities
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Address reversible causes (B12/thyroid), manage vascular risk factors, cognitive stimulation and physical activity; medication review (avoid anticholinergics)
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Underdiagnosis and limited access to memory clinics and neuropsychological testing outside capitals
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Growing awareness and structured chronic disease management in APS that can support risk-factor control
